Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows.

As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels, millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis.

Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims.

The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia, which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC.

Armed bandits in north-west Nigeria abducted dozens of villagers whom they invited to a meeting about potential peace negotiations, authorities and residents said on Monday, highlighting the region’s worsening security.

According to local police, 39 people were seized on Sunday during a meeting in the forest near Magamin Diddi village in the Maradun municipality of north-west Zamfara state. But some residents and officials believe the number of those abducted could be as high as 50.

events.title: Jamaica’s beach access crisis: ‘We shouldn’t be forced to fight for what is already ours’
events.summary:
Activists argue business model is ‘plantation tourism’ designed to benefit elite and disadvantage most Jamaicans

  • Campaigners go to court to fight privatisation of Jamaican coast

Devon Taylor remembers when the Mammee Bay shoreline in St Ann, Jamaica, was filled with children frolicking in the ocean after school, fishers haggling with locals over the price of their daily catch and craft vendors carving souvenirs under almond trees.

“I grew up on Mammee Bay,” Taylor says. He recalls fetching seawater in bottles for his grandmother when she was no longer able to go to the beach, learning to swim in the shallows, and watching generations of fishers cast their nets. “That beach raised us. It fed us.”

In a post on the Chinese platform WeChat on Friday, the ministry warned that an “invisible secret war” was quietly playing out in the seas around China as foreign agencies were collecting sensitive data “through a variety of new spying devices” to produce underwater maps that pose a “serious threat to our national security”.

Look, the direction of travel I think is an important one for us to follow through. He urged Iranians not to sit quietly.

Continue reading...

events.title: Growing risk that thousands buried in Gaza’s rubble may never be identified, says Red Cross
events.summary:
Exclusive: Recovery efforts remain slow and passing of time makes it more likely they will be skeletonised

The International Committee of the Red Cross has said the risk that the thousands of Palestinians buried beneath Gaza’s rubble may never be identified is increasing by the day, as recovery efforts remain slow and many victims have yet to be retrieved, the Guardian can reveal.

“There is no doubt that these bodies could soon become difficult to identify,” said Pat Griffiths, the ICRC spokesperson in Jerusalem. “The longer it takes for human remains to be recovered, the more difficult it can be to identify them. The longer the deceased lie beneath the rubble, the more likely they will be in advanced stages of decomposition – even skeletonised – when eventually recovered.”

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump says Iran peace deal could be signed by Sunday, with strait of Hormuz to open shortly after
events.summary:
US president says in online post he reserves ‘ultimate alternative’ if Tehran refuses to sign agreement

Donald Trump said on Saturday that the US is set to sign a new agreement with Iran the following day, claiming that the deal would prevent Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon, as well as reopen the strait of Hormuz to international shipping.

In a Truth Social post, Trump said that Iran “no longer want a Nuclear Weapon, nor will they have one, either through purchase, development, or any other form of procurement”.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘I only want justice’: bereaved families seek closure one year on from Air India crash
events.summary:
Relatives of those killed on flight AI171 are still struggling to obtain answers about what happened

When Sagar Patel’s mother boarded Air India flight AI171 on 12 June last year, she called her son as she always did before takeoff. The flight was due to leave Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel airport in Ahmedabad, in the western Indian state of Gujarat, and was destined for Gatwick.

“We always had a little traditional thing,” said Patel, a business manager from London. “Once she got on the flight, she would sit down and call me. She’d tell me: ‘Yep, I’m on the flight. See you later.’”

Continue reading...

events.title: US lawmakers demand Trump officials halt plan to send Afghans to DRC
events.summary:
Government urged to reconsider proposal for 1,100 Afghans, currently stranded in Qatar, who worked with US forces

Dozens of US lawmakers urged the Trump administration on Thursday to roll back any plans to ship to unsafe third countries Afghan nationals who worked with US forces during the war in their homeland.

In a letter seen by Reuters, more than 80 House of Representatives members, including at least three Republicans as well as Democrats, appealed to secretary of state, Marco Rubio, to reconsider plans for 1,100 Afghans who have been stranded in Qatar awaiting relocation.

Continue reading...

events.title: Delhi issues ‘strong protest’ after US strikes kill three Indian seafarers in Gulf
events.summary:
Washington claims vessel was violating its blockade of Iranian ports and failed to comply with instructions

The Indian government has voiced a “strong protest” after three Indian seafarers were killed in US military strikes against oil tankers travelling through the strait of Hormuz.

US Central Command confirmed that its aircraft had fired two Hellfire missiles at the engine room of the MT Settebello as it sailed through the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday.

Continue reading...

events.title: US diplomat found dead in Myanmar as police treat case as possible homicide
events.summary:
Thai woman is in custody in connection with investigation after man was reportedly found dead at a hotel in Yangon

A US diplomat has been found dead in Myanmar’s largest city, and members of the diplomatic community in Yangon say a Thai woman has been detained by police in connection with the investigation.

US officials in Thailand and the US embassy in Myanmar referred questions on the case to the state department, which confirmed the “death of a US government employee” assigned to the embassy in Yangon, but gave no other details.

Continue reading...

events.title: Two killed in rare street demonstration over women’s rights in Afghanistan
events.summary:
A child reported among those killed when Taliban forces fired on crowds in Herat, who were protesting over arrests of women accused of violating hijab dress code

A Taliban crackdown on women’s dress code in Afghanistan has escalated into a rare mass street protest in the western province of Herat, with at least two people, including one boy, killed by security forces.

Officials made a wave of arrests in recent days targeting women and young girls accused of “improper hijab”. Residents say many families had received no information about the whereabouts or condition of those detained.

Continue reading...

events.title: New defence secretary to ‘reprioritise’ UK plan for military spending, say sources
events.summary:
Starmer ‘can’t sack him or let him resign’, says ally of Dan Jarvis after predecessor’s resignation

The new defence secretary is to revisit a controversial plan for funding the armed forces and may return to demand more cash from the Treasury, allies have said.

Multiple government sources said Dan Jarvis would look to “reprioritise” aspects of the defence investment plan (Dip), which was delayed until July after the resignation of John Healey following a disagreement over its funding.

Continue reading...

events.title: Starmer to announce ‘Australia plus’ ban on social media for under-16s
events.summary:
Sources say hardline measures will also prevent young users from being able to talk to strangers on gaming apps

  • Why is the UK launching an ‘Australia plus’ social media ban and how will it work?

Keir Starmer is to ban under-16s from major social media apps such as TikTok, Instagram and X in sweeping restrictions described as “Australia plus”, the Guardian understands.

In a major policy shift far tougher than previously briefed, the prime minister will announce that teenagers will be banned from all the main social platforms. Online products that are not covered by the ban – such as gaming apps – will face new restrictions such as having the option to chat to strangers removed.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘A giant of the Labour movement’: Roy Hattersley, former Labour deputy leader, dies aged 93
events.summary:
Labour grandee was MP for Birmingham Sparkbrook from 1964 until his retirement from the Commons in 1997

Roy Hattersley, the former Labour deputy leader and author, has died at the age of 93.

Keir Starmer described Hattersley as a “giant of the labour movement”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Labour MP Lauren Edwards to bring assisted dying bill back to the Commons
events.summary:
MP for Rochester and Strood to use private member’s bill to put issue before MPs again after it was blocked by the Lords

The assisted dying bill is set to return to the Commons after the Labour MP Lauren Edwards agreed to use her private member’s bill to put the issue before MPs again.

Edwards said she wanted to give the legislation another chance because it had been blocked by the House of Lords after being passed by MPs. The return of the bill would give supporters a chance to use the Parliament Acts to potentially bypass the Lords if it was blocked for a second time.

Continue reading...

events.title: Police arrest 14 at rival protests over Israeli property event in London
events.summary:
About 1,000 people demonstrated for and against show in synagogue promoting sale of property in Israel

About 1,000 people protested for and against a show promoting the sale of property in Israel on Sunday, with police making 14 arrests.

Those opposed to the event, which was held in a north-west London synagogue, claimed it was also selling property in land illegally occupied by Israel and was part of an expansionist plan to drive out Palestinians. Organisers denied this was the case.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Reeks of corruption’: protesters rally as Trump hosts UFC event on his birthday
events.summary:
Demonstrations took off in Washington and across US as Trump throws first private, for-profit sporting event ever held on White House grounds

Dozens of people stood across the entrance gates to the Ellipse, the park south of the White House, on Sunday afternoon, holding protest signs and chanting as the president prepared to host seven mixed martial arts fights on the lawn.

Thousands of fight fans streamed past the protesters into the sprawling public viewing area that the Trump administration and the Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC), which is hosting the fights, erected steps from the White House. The cage fights, marketed as a celebration of the country’s “fighting spirit” ahead of its 250th anniversary, are being held on Donald Trump’s 80th birthday.

Continue reading...

events.title: JD Vance says he will discuss 2028 presidential run after midterm elections
events.summary:
Vice-president says he tries not to make decisions until he ‘absolutely must’ but has ‘no doubt’ Trump will support him

JD Vance said that he will discuss a 2028 US presidential run with his wife after the 2026 midterms.

The US vice-president gave insight into his ongoing decision on whether to run during an interview with CBS Sunday Morning where he spoke on his new memoir, Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith, which details his conversion to Catholicism.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pilot and 11 skydiving passengers killed in Missouri plane crash
events.summary:
Crash reportedly occurred shortly after departure from Butler Memorial airport at 11.30am on Sunday

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email

Twelve people were killed in a plane crash near Butler, Missouri, on Sunday.

According to Bates county emergency management, a private plane that had departed Butler Memorial airport shortly before 11.30am turned back before crashing near Business 49 Highway, Fox 4 reported.

Continue reading...

events.title: Mitch McConnell receiving medical care after being admitted to hospital
events.summary:
US senator is getting ‘excellent care’ after being hospitalized on Sunday morning, spokesperson says

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email

Mitch ⁠McConnell, a US senator ⁠from Kentucky, was admitted ​to ‌the ‌hospital ‌on Sunday morning, ‌a ​spokesperson ⁠said ​in ​a statement.

“Senator McConnell was admitted to the hospital this morning. He is receiving excellent care,” the statement read.

Continue reading...

events.title: Minnesota woman rescued after being trapped in mud pit for several days
events.summary:
Two friends found Kathryn Woessner, 68, in wooded area almost entirely submerged in mud puddle

A woman has been rescued from a mud pit in Minnesota after becoming trapped for several days.

On 6 June, two friends, Adam Sandbeck and Mike Gravalin, were riding their all-terrain vehicles through a wooded area near Backus and Hackensack in northern Minnesota when they discovered Kathryn Woessner, 68, almost entirely submerged in a mud puddle.

Continue reading...

events.title: G7 leaders face wars abroad and divisions at home
events.summary: President Trump and other G7 leaders meet in the French Alps this week as wars, economic uncertainty and strains in the transatlantic alliance loom over the summit.

events.title: As Iranians wait for a peace agreement, their economy is fraying
events.summary: Many Iranians say they can no longer afford the lives they once had. Writer and historian Arash Azizi talks with NPR's Adrian Florido about the economic pressures reshaping daily life.

events.title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia's shadow fleet
events.summary: Britain is investigating a sanctioned tanker that is suspected of being part of the Russian "shadow fleet," shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow's war on Ukraine.

events.title: Trump condemns Israeli strike in Beirut, warning attacks threaten deal on U.S-Iran war
events.summary: Trump said Israel's attack on Beirut Sunday "should not have happened," but maintained that the U.S. was still "very close to a Deal" to end the war with Iran.

events.title: World Cup online betting takes a toll in Brazil
events.summary: A World Cup fever is sweeping Brazil. So is sports betting. NPR's Julia Carneiro reports on the country's gambling boom and the growing concerns about debt and addiction.

events.title: A Lebanon town's grief in the aftermath of a deadly Israeli airstrike
events.summary: More than 3,700 people in Lebanon have died in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. In a village in southern Lebanon, one airstrike last month killed 14 people, including 10 women and children.

events.title: An exiled Russian journalist starts new career as a comedian
events.summary: Exiled Russian journalist Vladimir Raevsky says laughter can be a weapon against authoritarianism. NPR's Michele Kelemen reports on his history-inspired comedy act.

events.title: Trump travels to France for the 52nd G7 Summit
events.summary: President Trump heads to France for the G7 as the war in Iran continues to dominate global discussions.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid
events.summary: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

events.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
events.summary: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

events.title: Iran war: Pakistan announces US-Iran peace deal reached
events.summary: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif has announced a peace deal between the US and Iran, with a signing ceremony on June 19. Sharif claimed both sides would terminate military operations, including in Lebanon. DW has more.

events.title: Manuel Neuer: The aura of a record-breaking goalkeeper
events.summary: Germany's hopes at the 2026 World Cup are connected to world-class goalkeeper Manuel Neuer's fitness and form.

events.title: Germany start 2026 World Cup with win over Curacao
events.summary: For the first time since they won the tournament in 2014, Germany won their opening game at the World Cup.

events.title: Police fire tear gas at anti-G7 protesters in Geneva
events.summary: Protesters in Geneva clashed with police, set a car on fire and smashed the windows of a bank to show their opposition to the G7 summit taking place across the border in France.

events.title: Trump hosts White House cage fights to mark 80th birthday
events.summary: The US president is set to watch seven fights with an audience of 4,000 people inside a specially built arena on the White House lawn. Critics say the unprecendented spectacle is in poor taste amid the ongoing Iran war.

events.title: UK seizes suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker
events.summary: UK forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned Russian ship, with navy commandos rappelling onboard from helicopters. The UK and Ukraine hailed the move, saying it robbed Russia of money for its ongoing war of aggression.

events.title: Ukraine targets Russia's industrial facilities in massive strike
events.summary: Unverified footage on social media showed that a key chemical plant in Russia may have been hit. Meanwhile, the Uk has intercepted a Russian shadow fleet tanker.

events.title: Sahel juntas are 'crushing' basic freedoms
events.summary: In the Sahel countries of Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger, military rulers are increasingly clamping down on press freedom and freedom of expression. Many journalists, bloggers and activists have been forced into exile.

events.title: Why is Bulgaria banning arms supplies to Ukraine?
events.summary: Bulgaria's new government has banned state arms supplies to Ukraine. The ban does not, however, affect commercial exports, raising the question as to whether the move was aimed more at a domestic audience than at Kyiv.

events.title: Swiss voters reject population cap proposal
events.summary: A proposal to introduce a population cap of 10 million in the country has failed. It was put forward by a hard-right party.

events.title: New York Knicks win first NBA championship in 53 years
events.summary: Series MVP Jalen Brunson has led the New York Knicks to another comeback victory over the San Antonio Spurs. The win secured the franchise's third NBA championship, and its first since 1973.

events.title: Manuel Neuer fit to join Germany in first World Cup match
events.summary: Veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer is set to play in Germany's first match in this year's World Cup. He had retired from the national team in August 2024.

events.title: Germany news: President Steinmeier heads to Asia
events.summary: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier is kicking off a trip to Asia. And veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer will take to the field with the national squad in Germany's first match of the 2026 World Cup. DW has more.

events.title: The creatives trying to fix Germany's bureaucracy
events.summary: Berlin played host to the 9th annual Creative Bureaucracy Festival this week, where highly-motivated public service workers explained why a healthy democracy needs a functioning state.

events.title: Italy: Thousands attend anti- and pro-migration rallies in Rome
events.summary: Tens of thousands of people demonstrated in the Italian capital for rival protests. The rallies took place after a proposed bill seeking measures against migrants secured enough support to be brought before parliament.

events.title: Mexico: Mayor shot dead in Oaxaca state
events.summary: A mayor has died after being shot in an attack in southern Mexico, prosecutors said. Violence and insecurity are in the spotlight again in Mexico, which is co-hosting the FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Belfast: Thousands denounce violence at anti-racism rally
events.summary: Protesters condemned the scenes of anti-immigrant violence seen earlier in the week, which were in response to an alleged knife attack by a Sudanese refugee.

events.title: Fact check: These FIFA World Cup ticket sites are fake
events.summary: Desperate to get hold of tickets for the football World Cup, many fans face being scammed by sites set up to sell fake tickets. Here's how to make sure you don't fall into the trap.

events.title: Nigeria: Abducted former general dies in captivity
events.summary: Rabe Abubakar was abducted along with his wife by so-called bandits in the northwest. A government statement said he died from complications related to diabetes and hypertension.

events.title: Gold rush atmosphere at Berlin's military air trade show
events.summary: This year's ILA Berlin Air Show is breaking records, primarily because of the booming defense sector, which is reaping billions in contracts as a result of wars and crises.

events.title: Germany faces EU penalty over gender pay gap directive
events.summary: Germany was required to incorporate the EU Pay Transparency Directive into its national law, but it has missed the deadline.

events.title: A major submarine deal between India and Germany reflects New Delhi's push for military self-reliance — and Berlin's interest in the Indo-Pacific.
events.summary: A multibillion-dollar submarine deal between India and Germany reflects New Delhi's push for military self-reliance — and Berlin's growing interest in the Indo-Pacific.

events.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
events.summary: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
events.summary: SpaceX floated on the Nasdaq stock exchange on Friday, with shares trading 25% above the opening price at one point. The IPO has made Musk the world's first trillionaire, at least on paper.

events.title: German students up in arms about funding cuts
events.summary: Germany's research minister, Dorothee Bär, says the situation of German students is "very privileged" and has rejected reform of the Federal Training Assistance Act (BAföG), thereby turning many students against her.

events.title: Fast-track border checks, return centers, 'mandatory solidarity': What the EU’s new asylum rules could change — and what they may not.
events.summary: Germany aims to further reduce the number of new asylum seekers. The reformed EU asylum law (CEAS) came into effect on Friday, but experts say it is unlikely to achieve all it sets out to do.

events.title: Will life improve for Hungary's Roma community under Magyar?
events.summary: Under Viktor Orban, the Roma community was kept at the very bottom of the social ladder. His successor is taking a stand against antiziganism. But can Hungarian Roma expect fundamental changes?

events.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
events.summary: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

events.title: Jonathan Tah's first World Cup and the long wait
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup will be the Bayern Munich defender's first for Germany. This moment has been a long time coming.

events.title: Julian Nagelsmann: The coach struggling to speak to Germany
events.summary: Considered German football's X factor during Euro 2024, head coach Julian Nagelsmann enters the 2026 World Cup with more questions than optimism.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather bag fails to sell at auction
events.summary: The handbag promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product" didn't find a buyer at auction. Is it more chicken than dinosaur?

events.title: Environment wins of the week
events.summary: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply but it's out there. Each week DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

events.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico City
events.summary: Mexico won against South Africa after the opening ceremony in Mexico City which featured Colombian star Shakira and Nigerian singer Burna Boy. Football fans are set for nearly 40 days of action across three countries.

events.title: Racist riots break out across United Kingdom
events.summary: A video of a knife attack sparked racist riots across the Northern Irish capital Belfast on Monday, with violence has now spreading across the United Kingdom. Keir Starmer says there will be "no tolerance" for rioters.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.summary: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: UK: Defense Secretary John Healey quits in military spending dispute
events.summary: Britain's defense minister has abruptly quit his post, saying the government's plans to bolster defense spending in the coming years are inadequate. The move puts additional pressure on embattled UK PM Keir Starmer.

events.title: As the world heats up, cities work to cool down
events.summary: Global warming trends mean more frequent, intense heat waves. Cities around the world are working to mitigate extreme heat and adapt urban life to climate change.

events.title: The strongest El Nino in more than a century may be coming
events.summary: Warnings of a powerful El Nino weather pattern this year suggest that we may be about to face drought, flooding and extreme heat. What impacts are in store? And can we prepare for the worst of it?

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.summary: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Sex doll art sparks debate at German president's residence
events.summary: Beyond a headline-grabbing bronze, contemporary artworks draw attention to political power at an exhibition held at Bellevue Palace before the state building closes for renovation.

events.title: Hungary makes major deal with Ukraine, its former enemy
events.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Magyar announced an agreement with Kyiv about rights for a Hungarian minority in Ukraine. It paves the way for Ukraine to start EU accession talks — unthinkable under Hungary's last PM Orban.

events.title: World Cup 2026: How an old song from Bosnia went viral
events.summary: Some have already called it the unofficial song of the 2026 World Cup. So how did a tune that was more than a decade old take the Balkans — and beyond — by storm?

events.title: Neo-Nazi lost an east German election, but extremism remains
events.summary: A neo-Nazi candidate surprisingly lost a mayoral election by a small margin in the eastern state of Saxony. Many are relieved. But one win doesn't make the extremism problem go away.

events.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
events.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

events.title: World Cup: Edin Dzeko still strong for Bosnia-Herzegovina
events.summary: With so much unpleasantness surrounding this year's World Cup it's easy to forget that there's anything about it that can elicit a smile. But the story of Bosnia-Herzegovina captain Edin Dzeko sure can.

events.title: War in Ukraine: Kyiv strikes key Russian supply lines
events.summary: Russian troops rely on supplies via the land corridor to Crimea, but Ukrainian forces are now able to launch concentrated attacks along the critical route. Experts say this indicates growing Russian vulnerability.

events.title: Why pastors hesitate to talk climate in US churches
events.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental protections, some Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land. But many others stay silent.

events.title: NASA picks first European astronaut for Artemis mission
events.summary: Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano will be the lead pilot for the Artemis III test flight. The NASA mission is expected to launch in 2027 to test technology for a future moon landing.

events.title: Northern Ireland: Police urge calm after 'sickening' Belfast stabbing
events.summary: Belfast police appealed for calm after a violent knife attack caught on videophone prompted calls for anti-immigration protests. This followed a week of migration-related riots last June in Northern Ireland.

events.title: EU proposes entry ban for Russians who fought in Ukraine
events.summary: The EU's 21st sanctions package includes a provision to deny entry to anyone who has served in Russia's armed forces amid the invasion of Ukraine. It will also target the so-called shadow fleet, banks and cryptocurrency.

events.title: Leipzig Bachfest 2026: Bach's music as hit parade
events.summary: The Leipzig Bach Festival honors the composer with a hit parade. Over 8,000 fans from 20 countries cast their votes. But Bach is celebrated in many other countries around the world as well.

events.title: From Russia to Israel to US, 'warlords' seize power — report
events.summary: Russia invades Ukraine, Israel and the US attack Iran, civil wars drag on in Africa — and what about international law? German researchers paint a grim picture in their latest annual Peace Report.

events.title: Germany upbeat as final World Cup preparations begin
events.summary: As the German men's national football team embark on their final preparations for the World Cup at their base camp in North Carolina, they're looking to make amends for disappointing tournaments in Russia and Qatar.

events.title: Why Anthropic has the edge over OpenAI in IPO race
events.summary: The rivalry between Sam Altman and Dario Amodei is entering a new phase. Both AI bosses are planning public listings and hope to use the money to outpace each other in the race to build superintelligent AI.

events.title: Will Ebola have an impact on the World Cup?
events.summary: While ticket prices, heat and politics have made headlines, Ebola has been a World Cup worry for DR Congo. The African nation has qualified for the first time in 52 years, but does the outbreak have wider implications?

events.title: Somali referee denied US entry, dropped from FIFA World Cup 2026
events.summary: Omar Artan, Africa's top referee in 2025, was set to become the first Somali national to officiate at a FIFA World Cup before being denied entry by US authorities

events.title: Franco-German fighter jet project collapses after industry dispute
events.summary: The next-generation aircraft project has been scrapped following company disagreements, dealing a blow to European security cooperation efforts.

events.title: Will Mexico's World Cup party be spoiled by teacher protest?
events.summary: Just before the World Cup kicks off, host country Mexico faces a political tug-of-war. Striking teachers have occupied the central fan zone and have pledged to continue protesting.

events.title: Depression in romantic relationships: You, me and the illness
events.summary: Depression can be a huge challenge for a relationship. Loved ones of someone with depression often feel helpless. But there is actually a lot they can do ― and a lot they shouldn't.

events.title: Why the 2026 World Cup is so controversial
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup has come under fire even before kickoff. Criticism is mounting over US President Donald Trump’s travel bans, high ticket prices, the expansion to 48 teams as well as its environmental impact.

events.title: Jafar Panahi's prison sentence confirmed by Iranian court
events.summary: Following a retrial for a verdict that had been reached while the prominent Iranian director was abroad, the Revolutionary Court in Tehran has upheld a one-year jail sentence.

events.title: Kosovo PM wins snap vote, but with no majority
events.summary: Sunday's vote, the country's third in just over a year, was marked by low turnout. The country's prolonged political crisis has further debilitated an already ailing economy.

events.title: Zelenskyy angers Poland with WWII-era name for army unit
events.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has sparked outrage in Poland by choosing a name that carries Nazi connotations for a Ukrainian army unit. Can Polish PM Donald Tusk smooth things over?

events.title: 'Pizza connection' between German conservatives and Greens
events.summary: Thirty years ago, rivaling politicians from Germany's conservative CDU and the Greens met to discuss their differences ― and what they had in common. The pizza connection was born. Now that format is being revived.

events.title: Sweden mulls prison for young teens as violent crime rises
events.summary: In the fight against escalating gang crime, Sweden's parliament will vote on plans to hold 13-year-olds criminally responsible and, in serious cases, imprison them. Other European nations are also lowering age limits.

events.title: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future
events.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

events.title: Germany beat USA before World Cup but injuries cast shadow
events.summary: Germany’s final match before their World Cup start ended with a 2-1 win. But a tournament-ending injury to rising star Lennart Karl and doubts about veteran Manuel Neuer mean coach Julian Nagelsmann has work to do.

events.title: High-functioning depression: When success masks suffering
events.summary: People with depression don't always feel overwhelmed or struggle to get things done — some are highly efficient and productive, despite their low moods. So it can't be that bad, right? Wrong!

events.title: Hunger in Oleshky: Ukraine asks for evacuation
events.summary: The Ukrainian city of Oleshky has been occupied by Russian troops since 2022. Many residents are cut off from the outside world and living in dire conditions. Now, Kyiv wants to rescue them.

events.title: Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days
events.summary: The war waged by the US and Israel against Iran has now lasted 100 days. In that time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has drastically changed his stance on the war.

events.title: Successive elections take a hit on Kosovo's coffers
events.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia, and obstacles to EU integration.

events.title: Why businesses are offering 'Quiet Hour'
events.summary: More peace and quiet, less stress — the Quiet Hour is an initiative for people with invisible disabilities. An increasing number of stores and institutions in Germany are launching similar campaigns.

events.title: Slovenia's political survivor Janez Jansa is back
events.summary: The new coalition government of Slovenia's veteran leader Janez Jansa is already advancing divisive policies that some warn could deepen polarization.

events.title: Environment wins of the week
events.summary: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply, but it's out there. Each week, DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

events.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
events.summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

events.title: What are Europe's alternatives to Instagram, TikTok and X?
events.summary: As criticism of Instagram, TikTok and X grows in the EU, developers have been working on European social media apps. But can they compete? DW answers the key questions.

events.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
events.summary: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

events.title: EU seeking ways to speed up Western Balkans membership
events.summary: European Council President Antonio Costa said new ways need to be found to speed up Western Balkans membership in the EU. His comments come ahead of an upcoming summit in Montenegro.

events.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
events.summary: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

events.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
events.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

events.title: Germany's UN defeat: What went wrong?
events.summary: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

events.title: Can Hawaii wean itself off oil imports?
events.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

events.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
events.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

events.title: Kushner-backed Albania resort sparks protests, EU concerns
events.summary: Donald Trump's son-in-law is linked to a megaproject on Albania's protected coast that has triggered protests and EU scrutiny over risks to flamingos and endangered habitats.

events.title: 2026 World Cup: 6 things you need to know
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup is to be bigger than all of its predecessors. Whether it will be better is an entirely different question. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

events.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
events.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

events.title: Germany sees record number of discrimination claims in 2025
events.summary: The federal commissioner for anti-discrimination has said the government's reform plans, the first in 20 years, are inadequate and won't provide the resources and funding needed to combat discrimination.

events.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
events.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from Wim Wenders' film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Backrooms: The creepy internet phenomenon, explained
events.summary: A low-budget horror film directed by a 20-year-old YouTuber is beating the new Star Wars movie at the box office. Behind its success is an entire viral online maze.

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
events.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

events.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
events.summary: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

events.title: Unpacking Germany's campaign for a UN Security Council seat
events.summary: On Wednesday, the United Nations will hold elections for several seats on the UN Security Council. Germany's government is campaigning vigorously for a seat.

events.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
events.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows and even to have killed their male offspring. What is the history behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

events.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
events.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

events.title: Is 'nation-hopping' ruining German youth football?
events.summary: Many young German footballers are choosing other countries over Germany. DFB Director Andreas Rettig is aiming to change this and is calling for a compensation fee.

events.title: Mozart the idol: Searching for beauty in times of crisis
events.summary: Mozart's music evokes lightness and beauty. In times of war and destruction, people often invoke the ideals of the classical era. But is this just an escape from reality? The Mozartfest Würzburg offers different answers.

events.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
events.summary: Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time since 2019. Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

events.title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
events.summary: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

events.title: German town prints its own cash to cut CO2 emissions
events.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
events.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
events.summary: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

events.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
events.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

events.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
events.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

events.title: Swing Youth: In Nazi Germany, jazz was an act of defiance
events.summary: The Nazis denounced jazz music as "degenerate art," despite its widespread popularity in Germany. As the Nazis clamped down on expression, groups of jazz-loving teenagers formed the Swing Youth to rebel.

events.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
events.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

events.title: PSG's team of talents retain Champions League title
events.summary: Paris Saint-Germain were unable to score from open play against a resilient Arsenal but won their second successive title on penalties. Luis Enrique has built a team to be proud of at the oil-rich club.

events.title: Film funding bill to support blockbusters made in Germany
events.summary: The German government wants to attract more filmmakers and make the country a "global player" in on-screen entertainment. A new bill would require media companies to make mandatory investments in the film industry.

events.title: Germany: Vaping becoming more popular among young people
events.summary: A recent study shows that e-cigarettes are becoming increasingly popular among young people in Germany, with even elementary school kids picking up vapes. Health experts are alarmed.

events.title: International aid cuts complicate Congo's Ebola epidemic
events.summary: The scaling back of development aid has contributed to the inability to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. German organizations are calling for a major spending boost to help stop the spread.

events.title: What makes a vanity project? A history of building for power
events.summary: As Donald Trump proposes costly redesigns for Washington, DC, the question arises: When do grand projects serve the public — and when do they serve the leader?

events.title: Kai Havertz: Arsenal and Germany's man for the big moment
events.summary: Germany forward Kai Havertz is set to play in his second Champions League final. The Arsenal man is a match winner.

events.title: Germany deepens Taiwan ties amid China tensions
events.summary: A German delegation, known as the Berlin-Taipei Parliamentary Friendship Group, recently visited Taiwan, drawing criticism from Beijing. Two lawmakers told DW the visit was important for expanding economic partnerships.

events.title: Pigeons' livers may enable magnetic navigation when the sun doesn't shine, study finds
events.summary: A research team in Germany has published new data on how pigeons use magnetic fields as a navigation method when visibility is limited. A key part of the long-sought answer could lie in an unexpected organ, their livers.

events.title: The Superbug spill: When medicine goes rogue
events.summary: Pharmaceuticals have been found in waterways almost everywhere in the world, fueling antibiotic-resistant superbugs that no longer respond to treatment. Is there a way out?

events.title: Suez, Panama canals charge for transit — why can't Hormuz?
events.summary: Iran has been widely condemned for demanding up to $2 million for vessels to ship through the Strait of Hormuz. DW explores the reasons why Egypt can charge for the Suez Canal and Panama for its waterway, but Iran can't.

events.title: Reiche brings energy sector ties to German Economy Ministry
events.summary: Economy Minister Reiche is a controversial figure. Her plans for new gas-fired power plants, rolling back plans for more renewables in the building sector and scrapping solar subsidies have been met with criticism.

events.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
events.summary: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

events.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
events.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: New York and New Jersey prosecutors probe 'sky-high' ticket prices
events.summary: US prosecutors have subpoenaed FIFA over expensive World Cup tickets, saying the event is "not an invitation to exploit our residents and visitors."

events.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage
events.summary: Solar and wind produce lots of energy — but not always at the right time. More battery storage could help Europe to stabilize prices and replace polluting fossil fuel energy, but roadblocks remain.

events.title: Germany eyes heat pumps as Iran war drives energy costs up
events.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and causing energy prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

events.title: License to play: The new James Bond video game
events.summary: James Bond is back, and he's as charismatic and daring as ever. The video game "007 First Light" tells the origin story of the British secret agent — and the stakes are high.

events.title: The German gym fighting the far right with MMA
events.summary: The far right is increasingly using MMA to recruit new members. But a gym in Chemnitz, in the east of Germany, is fighting back with a martial arts program focused on diversity and empowerment.

events.title: How did Berlin's Jewish Hospital and hundreds of its patients survive the Holocaust?
events.summary: When Red Army troops entered Berlin's Jewish Hospital in 1945, they were astonished to discover hundreds of Jews living just a couple of miles away from Hitler's bunker.

events.title: Ferrari unveils its first all-electric car, the 4-door Luce
events.summary: The Italian supercar giant has released images of what will be both its first ever pure EV, and its first car that can seat five people. The Luce eyes a younger clientele and the increasingly competitive Chinese market.

events.title: The wind boom Trump couldn't stop
events.summary: Even with fierce opposition, offshore wind projects are multiplying along US coasts — signaling a massive shift in energy.

events.title: Berlin Hauptbahnhof at 20: A rail hub with symbolic power
events.summary: Berlin's central station was inaugurated in May 2006. Beyond its functionality, the modern glass-and-steel structure was built to play a key role in reunified Germany.

events.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
events.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

events.title: Blinded by the light pollution: Cities seek to restore night
events.summary: Artificial lights are blotting out the moon and stars, harming human health, and disrupting the natural world. Why is this a problem? And how can we stop it without sitting in the dark?

events.title: Enhanced Games: Why 'crippling injuries' are the least of it
events.summary: Doping in sports is nothing new. Athletes at the Enhanced Games will know the risks — severe injury, even paralysis. But bioethicists are also concerned we're opening the gates to altering our fundamental human biology.

events.title: What is Pentecost?
events.summary: People new to Germany are often surprised by the number of public holidays in the spring. Pentecost is one of them, but its significance is not well-known.

events.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump ally at the Federal Reserve?
events.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

events.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
events.summary: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

events.title: The dying dream of the dead to be at peace with life
events.summary: Many people have vivid dreams shortly before death. Research suggests the dreams are not a sign of confusion — but may help both the dying and their loved ones make sense of loss.

events.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
events.summary: An Ebola‑infected US doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

events.title: Will Germany's aviation tax cut lower ticket prices?
events.summary: The tax cut aims to ease the burden on the aviation industry and make flying slightly cheaper. However, some experts warn that it is unlikely to have a meaningful impact on ticket prices.

events.title: Germany 2026 World Cup squad announcement: Neuer returns
events.summary: Two years after retiring from international football, Manuel Neuer is back in Germany's 2026 World Cup squad.

events.title: Extreme climate scenario fades, but warming continues
events.summary: The rapid rollout of renewable energy is helping to shift emissions trends, but expected temperature rises remain high as the UN moves to tighten countries' commitments.

events.title: Freiburg's Europa League final proof another way can work
events.summary: Bundesliga side Freiburg might have lost their first-ever European final to Aston Villa, but their European run is a reminder that success in football can be achieved without big spending or a loss of identity.

events.title: Researchers spot humpback whales off Australia and Brazil — longest lifetime journey on record
events.summary: Researchers have observed two humpback whales swimming between Australia and Brazil. It's the longest travel distance humans have ever recorded for the species.

events.title: How Meta wants to profile 13-year-olds on Insta, Facebook
events.summary: Instagram and Facebook have a problem with underage users — there's too many of them. Parent company, Meta, aims to root out under-13s with an age-profiling AI. This plays into a debate about a teenage social media ban.

events.title: EU is failing to cut pesticide use
events.summary: With binding cut targets scrapped, the European Union's pledge to halve pesticide use is stalling — even as controversial chemicals like glyphosate remain on sale across the bloc.

events.title: Hearing aids may cut noise by reading brain signals
events.summary: In a crowded room, it can be impossible for hearing aid users to focus on what they want or need to hear. Research into auditory neurotechnology may enhance sounds and voices and reduce noise in real-time.

events.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
events.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

events.title: Germany's crisis-hit chemical industry seeks revival
events.summary: Hit by high energy costs and regulation, Germany's chemical companies are scaling back production at home while expanding abroad. Can the trend be reversed?

events.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
events.summary: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

events.title: Germany and Japan test hydrogen future with BMW, Toyota cars
events.summary: During a recent visit to Japan, Germany's transport minister Patrick Schnieder toured hydrogen projects aimed at bringing the fuel into the mainstream.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Netherlands let victory slip as Japan salvage point with last-gasp header
World Cup 2026: Japan deny Netherlands with last-gasp equaliser
events.summary: Netherlands and Japan ended their Group of Death encounter in a dramatic 2-2 draw after a thrilling spectacle in the second half. Daichi Kamada's stunning last-gasp header salvaged a point for the Samurai Blue, denying Oranje what appeared to be a crucial victory in a group where every point is precious.

events.title: Pakistan's prime minister says US and Iran have reached ceasefire deal
Iran’s World Cup team arrive in United States
events.summary: Iran’s World Cup team touched down on American soil on Sunday, arriving at Los Angeles International Airport after a short flight from their base camp in Mexico. This is the first time in the cup’s nearly 100-year history that a host nation receives a country it is at war with.

events.title: Netherlands - Japan: Dramatic late equaliser for Samurai Blue
events.summary: The Netherlands and Japan played out a thrilling 2-2 draw in a decisive Group of Death encounter that leaves both teams' knockout ambitions hanging in the balance. Japan came from behind twice but ultimately had to settle for a late equaliser through Daichi Kamada's stoppage-time header, denying Oranje a decisive victory. Read our liveblog to see how the match unfolded.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Germany overpower tournament debutant Curaçao
World Cup 2026: Germany overpower tournament debutant Curaçao
events.summary: Germany made a successful start to World Cup 2026 on Sunday, demolishing Curaçao 7-1 in Boston. The four-time world champions were clinical in their execution, with goals from Felix Nmecha, Nico Schlotterbeck, Kai Havertz, Jamal Musiala, Nathaniel Brown, and Deniz Undav. Despite the heavy defeat, Curaçao marked their historic World Cup debut with a goal of their own through Livano Comenencia.

events.title: Trump at 80: "Harry Houdini" & A Cage Fight
events.summary:
President Trump turns 80 today, and the White House lawn has been transformed into a giant arena, complete with floodlights, cameras, giant Star-Spangled Banners and a 90-foot scaffold known as “The Claw,” as 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue hosts “Freedom 250”, a celebration featuring professional UFC cage fighting.
 
The event combines three occasions: President Trump’s birthday, Flag Day, which marks the adoption of the U.S. flag in 1777, and the launch of celebrations leading up to the 250th anniversary of American independence.
 
Professor Jeffrey Sonnenfeld has known President Trump for 25 years and has critiqued his leadership style in the best selling book Trump’s Ten Commandments. He is also Senior Associate Dean for Leadership Studies at the Yale School of Management and has advised five U.S. presidents. He's been speaking to France 24's Gavin Lee.

events.title: Putin and Zelensky hold separate calls with Trump on his birthday
events.summary: Russia’s Vladimir Putin and Ukraine’s Volodomyr Zelensky on Sunday held separate calls with US President Donald Trump as the latter prepared to celebrate his 80th birthday. The leaders discussed the war in Ukraine and their perceptions on how the more then four-year conflict could be ended.

events.title: Tech 24 takes to the skies as VivaTech takes over the Champs-Elysées
events.summary: France’s biggest geek get-together started with a bang this year. For its 10th anniversary, the VivaTech trade show took to the Champs-Elysées, billing the installation as "Europe's largest open-air technology experience."

events.title: Swiss police fire tear gas as anti-G7 protesters target UN buildings
events.summary: Swiss police on Sunday fired tear gas at demonstrators protesting the upcoming Group of Seven summit in France after some protesters started targeting buildings linked to the United Nations in Geneva.

events.title: American musician Oliver Tree killed in helicopter crash in Brazil
events.summary: Oliver Tree, a US alt-pop singer and internet personality, died in a helicopter crash in Brazil on Sunday, a police source said. He was 32. Tree was on a world tour when the helicopter he was travelling in collided with another mid-air. A total of six people died in the incident, which left no survivors.

events.title: UK intercepts Russian shadow fleet vessel in Channel: ministry
events.summary: British forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned oil tanker belonging to Russia's shadow fleet in the English Channel, the defence ministry said, in a six-hour operation hailed by Kyiv.

events.title: Trump vows Mideast deal is on, calls for no more attacks
events.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday said Israeli strikes on Beirut "should not have happened" as he vowed a regional peace deal was at hand, though he did not confirm his earlier claim it would be signed during the day. France 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.

events.title: Donald Trump turns 80 and celebrates with UFC cage fighting on the White House lawn
events.summary: President Donald Trump turned 80 on Sunday and was set to celebrate with one of the more surreal spectacles in both sports and the nation's capital: cage fighting on the White House lawn. We discuss it with our international affairs commentator Douglas Herbert.

events.title: The week in pictures: World Cup 2026 kicks off, the Pope visits Spain and the White House prepares for a cage fight showdown
events.summary: The Pope visits Segrada Familia during his trip to Barcelona, the 2026 football World Cup kicks off in Mexico city, and the White House prepares for its first cage fight showdown. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.title: UK celebrates King Charles’s official birthday with Trooping the Colour
events.summary: Crowds have gathered in central London for the Trooping the Colour ceremony to mark King Charles III’s official birthday.

events.title: Beirut strikes: 'No point' in talks if US cannot uphold 'commitments'
events.summary: Iranian chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Sunday there was "no point" in continuing peace talks with the United States after its ally Israel attacked Beirut's southern suburbs.

events.title: Trump hosts White House cage fights amid war and political scrutiny
events.summary: Donald Trump hosts a card of seven mixed martial arts fights to be held on Sunday inside a specially built arena on the White House's South Lawn on his 80th birthday, a spectacle spotlighting his norm-defying style at a tense moment in his presidency. We discuss it with Ellen Kountz, Dean of the Finance Department at INSEEC Business School.

events.title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
events.summary: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut on Sunday despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war. Smoke could be seen rising over the Lebanese capital. We discuss the latest developments with our correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.

events.title: Brazil struggle against Morocco, Australia stun Turkiye
events.summary: Morocco looked impressive in their first game against Brazil, but Vinicius managed to bring it level with a beautiful goal. Switzerland also struggled against Qatar, despite being favourites to top their group. Scotland brought the vibes to their match against Haiti, and a deflected shot by McGuinn gave Scotland their first world cup win in 28 years. Unexpectedly, Australia won 2-0 against Turkiye.

events.title: 'Must-see event', rare sumo tournament kicks off in Paris
events.summary: A 150-strong delegation, two chartered planes... More than 30 years after their last visit to France, the elite of sumo wrestling – Japan's national and sacred sport – is making a highly anticipated return to Paris for a weekend showcase.

events.title: EU says to resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday
events.summary: The European Union will resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday, the bloc announced, after Hungary lifted the veto imposed by its previous pro-Russian leader.

events.title: G7 meeting begins in France this Monday
events.summary: U.S. ​President ​Donald Trump will meet with Middle ​Eastern ‌leaders ⁠and ‌attend a working session ⁠with Ukrainian President ​Volodymyr Zelenskiy ‌during the G7 summit ‌in France ​next week, senior U.S. administration officials ​said.

events.summary: Police in Geneva have fired tear gas and water cannons at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency in protests ahead of tomorrow's G7 summit in neighbouring France.

events.summary: US musician Oliver Tree is believed to be among six people dead after a mid-air collision between two helicopters in Rio de Janeiro.

events.title: British 1-2-3 at Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix - first since 1968
events.summary: Sir Lewis Hamilton has won his first Formula 1 Grand Prix victory with Ferrari, while George Russell and defending champion Lando Norris made it the first all-British podium since 1968.

events.summary: Switzerland has rejected a cap on the country's population when the proposal was put to the vote in a nationwide referendum.

events.title: Leader of Venezuelan gang killed in US strike, Trump says
events.summary: President Donald Trump has said a "swift and lethal kinetic" US military strike has killed "the infamous leader" of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua street gang.

events.summary: Around 200 protesters have torn down fencing surrounding a controversial luxury development in Albania, as demonstrations continue over another planned resort linked ​to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

events.summary: A rare, deep-sea goblin shark has been studied alive in its habitat for the first time.

events.summary: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha has died after nearly four years in a coma, aged 47.

events.summary: Two helicopters have collided over Rio de Janeiro, crashing in the city’s western zone and killing all six aboard

events.summary: Roberto Vannacci, a former Italian army general, is shaking up Italy's political scene with his new party, Futuro Nazionale

events.summary: Romanian President Nicusor Dan has nominated Adrian Vestea as prime minister to resolve a political crisis

events.summary: Taiwan’s intelligence agency says it is setting up a secure channel for Chinese nationals to offer information

events.summary: Shops and businesses in downtown Geneva have boarded up their storefronts ahead of planned anti-G7 protests on Sunday

events.summary: Mexican authorities are investigating the homicide of a mayor in San Miguel Amatitlan, Oaxaca

events.summary: Mexican authorities are investigating how a corpse ended up outside a stadium in the border city of Tijuana, where Iran’s national team has been training for its upcoming World Cup match against New Zealand

events.summary: The Trump administration killed Venezuelan gang leader El Niño Guerrero during an airstrike conducted by the U.S. military in southeastern Venezuela

events.summary: Tens of thousands of LGBTQ+ supporters march in Romania and Bulgaria for annual pride parades

events.summary: Tens of thousands of people marched in Rome in rival demonstrations over migration

events.summary: Thousands of demonstrators have gathered in Belfast to denounce anti-immigrant rioters

events.summary: Armed men in Haiti have kidnapped James Boyard, the cabinet director of the Defense Ministry and inspector general of Haiti’s police

events.summary: Gunmen have killed at least 17 farmers and wounded about 13 others in northwestern Nigeria

events.summary: Momentum for a deal to end the Iran war is growing

events.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack has killed one person and injured three in Russia’s Krasnodar region, according to local officials

events.summary: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

events.title: Deal reached between the United States and Iran, Trump says
events.summary: An agreement has been reached between the United States and Iran, President Donald Trump and Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced Sunday, with Iranian state media broadcasting the announcement.“The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete,” he said.

events.title: Qatari negotiators fly to Tehran in a push to finalize U.S.-Iran deal
events.summary: An effort is underway to finalize a U.S.-Iran deal, expected to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but the push to finalize a memorandum of understanding has been rattled by fresh Israeli strikes on Lebanon’s capital

events.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices

events.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live

events.title: Ceramicists spent 14 years working on the  cross atop the Sagrada Familia
events.summary: NBC News' Molly Hunter interviewed ceramicists Toni and Guillem who spent 14 years working on the 24-ton reflective cross atop the Sagrada Familia.

events.title: 2026 World Cup kicks off in Mexico as U.S. prepares to face off with Paraguay
events.summary: The first day of the 2026 World Cup kicked off in Mexico amid red cards and excitement. Mexico scored the first goal and victory of the tournament when they beat South Africa 2-0 on home soil in front of a packed stadium in Mexico City. NBC News' Morgan Chesky reports on the action from day one of the 2026 World Cup and what to expect from the U.S. national team.

events.title: Trump says deal with Iran could be signed in days
events.summary: President Trump said an agreement to end the war could be signed with the Iranian regime within days, but that some thorny issues would remain for later talks. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

events.title: Iranian state media says reports of a deal are 'speculative and nothing has been finalized'
events.summary: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.

events.title: Why Trump’s eyes are on Iran’s Kharg Island — and why that's a risk
events.summary: The island less than 20 miles off Iran accounts for more than 90% of the country's oil exports.

events.title: Woman dies after being thrown from bridge without secured bungee cord in Brazil

events.title: Armed men in Haiti’s capital seize a top security official in rare high-level abduction
events.summary: Armed men in Haiti have kidnapped James Boyard, cabinet director of the Defense Ministry and a highly respected security expert who also serves as inspector general of Haiti’s police, a person with the knowledge of the situation said Saturday
